perFile.notes: All 77 images viewed successfully (PNG/JPEG only; no .wdp/.wmf unviewable files). File is a compilation of teaching cards + tracing/activity worksheets for the first four letters in alphabetic order: ا(أ), ب, ت, ث. Many images (m001-m007, m017-m024, m026, m037-m042, m044, m046-m047, m073-m077) are standalone clipart used as vocab illustrations (lion, well, boy, mouse, glass/cup, cow, penguin, polar bear, dog, door, berries, whale, crocodile, tulips, teacher, etc.); several clipart files are duplicated at the end (m073=m001 lion, m074=m017 cow, m075 door, m076-m077 berries). Letter ا is presented as hamza-on-alif (أ) on most teaching cards (vocab أسد/أرنب) but plain alif (ا) appears in identification grids; recorded as 'ا' in letters[] per the canonical letter. m048 is an image-to-form strip showing a 3-dotted (ث-like) letter paired with apple/orange/blackberry pictures whose words start with ت/ب, not ث — likely a layout/clipart mismatch in the source; FLAG FOR MOTHER to confirm intended letter. Short-vowel (harakat) practice appears for ب (بَ بُ بِ) and ت (تَ تُ تِ). Vowel marks on isolated vocab words were not always legible and were normalized to standard spelling. No full sentences present; vocab is single words only. Stroke-order arrow diagrams present for أ, ت, ث (not a clear standalone one for ب beyond the dotted trace). Confidence high for the four target letters and the activity taxonomy; medium on a few individual vowel diacritics and the m048 mismatch.

perFile.title: اختر الصورة المناسبة للحرف
perFile.instruction_en: Choose the picture that matches the letter (the picture whose word begins with that letter).
perFile.notes: Cut-out matching cards: each card shows one Arabic letter beside a column of 3 small pictures; the child selects the picture whose word begins with the letter. Picture sets repeat across pairs of cards (same 3 images, different target letter), so the intended answer is the one image whose word starts with the card's letter. p001 cards: ك/ش (ball, sun, crab-or-seaweed -> شمس for ش), ح/ب (horse, cat, cow -> حصان/بقرة), د/ف (caterpillar, butterfly, squirrel -> دودة/فراشة). p002 cards: س/ك (turtle, cherry, bread -> سلحفاة/كرز), ب/م (lemon, banana, orange -> برتقال/موز), ل/أ (lemon, pineapple, pear -> ليمون/أناناس). The ك card on p001 and the bread image have no clearly matching answer among their shown pictures (low confidence on those specific pairings). Words inferred from pictures, not printed on the page. p003 is a blank white page (no content).

perFile.title: الحروف مع صور وكلمات
perFile.instruction_en: Letter teaching chart: each page shows one Arabic letter in the center with four example words, each paired with a picture (letter + picture + word).
perFile.notes: 29 pages, one Arabic letter per page (puzzle-card layout: center letter + 4 corner words each with a picture). Letters appear NOT in strict alphabetical order: page 1 is the hamza ء (center glyph ء; example words أرنب/فأر/ببغاء/بئر all contain hamza), then ب..ي in order, and the FINAL page 29 is bare alif ا (long-a, words فار/أفعى/عصا). Confirm with the mother whether page 1 should be classified as أ/همزة and whether placing ا last is intentional. Vowel diacritics (harakat) are inconsistent across pages and were not transcribed exhaustively. Low-confidence/ambiguous items flagged: p011 (ز) top-left shows أزرق with a blue OCTAGON while p010 (ر) shows أزرق with a blue CIRCLE — both rendered as color 'azraq'. p025 (ن) bottom-right word read as 'قن' (coop) but the picture is unclear (looks like a person/scene) — verify intended word. p014 (ص) and p020 (ف) both reuse عصفور (bird). Page colors used as vocab: أخضر (green), أزرق (blue), أسود (black). Some images are smaller/low-res scans (p001, p015, p021).

perFile.title: الكلمة والعكس
perFile.instruction_en: The word and its opposite — match each word with its antonym.
perFile.notes: Each PDF spread is a word↔opposite pair: green card (left) = word, pink card (right) = its opposite. 24 pairs across 24 page images, all read by sight. Pairs are stored word→opposite as the green-card term → pink-card term; the antonym relationship is symmetric, so order is presentational only. Page 19 (لَيل↔نَهار) rendered at smaller scale but text legible. Harakat (vowel marks) transcribed as shown; a few cards show partial/optional diacritics. مُبكي / مُضحك (p14) shown without full harakat on the card. Flag for the mother: confirm preferred standard vocalization for these adjectives, and whether يَنْزَل/يَصْعَد (verbs 'goes down'/'goes up') belong with the adjective opposites or in a separate verb set. signedOff:false.

Pictures identified by sight; Arabic words are INFERRED from the depicted objects, not read — flagged low confidence. Pictures consistent with target sound ت (placed in items, all word-initial): m002=crocodile=تمساح, m006=crown=تاج, m007=television=تلفاز (could be تلفزيون), m008=apple=تفاحة, m004=strawberries=توت (could be فراولة). Likely distractors WITHOUT ت (in vocab, not items): m001=fish=سمكة, m003=car=سيارة, m005=wristwatch=ساعة, m009=mouse=فأر. m004 ambiguous (توت vs فراولة). No .wdp/.wmf files present; all 9 images viewed. Cannot confirm the actual exercise instruction (circle/color/write) — title and worksheet kind drive the writeLetter mapping.

LOW-CONFIDENCE: m013 third fill-blank word read as رغيف but the picture (fire/launch) and partial text (..ـرِيفٌ) do not clearly match — Arabic vowel marks faint; flagged. No .wdp/.wmf files present (all PNG). No full sentences in this packet — only single words and letter forms.

perFile.notes: 18 images, all viewed. Primary worksheetType=traceLetter (dominant trace/write activity); mix of teach cards, coloring, find-the-letter, circle-the-letter, and short-vowel writing (دَ/دُ/دِ). Letter forms shown: isolated د and connected final form (ـد) in m014. Word forms span initial (دجاجة, دودة, دب, دراجة), medial (أسد), and final (قرد, صياد) positions. Letter ذ does NOT appear (only د). Teachers credited: Kaoutar/Kawthar, Abu Bakr Seddik school. No full sentences present. Arabic read by sight from images. Words دائرة and ديناصور appear only as small inline reading-row items (medium confidence on full diacritics). .wdp/.wmf: none present in folder.

perFile.notes: 16 images total; 12 PNG read by sight, 4 unviewable (.wmf: m005, m006, m015, m016) - flagged and skipped. Forms shown across worksheet: isolated ك, initial كـ, medial ـكـ, final ـك. Vowel diacritics on some vocab inferred from standard spelling - medium confidence on exact harakat.

Several embedded clip-art images (m003 butterfly, m004 fish, m006 Mickey-with-ball) do NOT contain the letter laam and look like generic coloring art rather than laam vocabulary — flag for the mother. Confident laam vocab from the sheet: ليمون (printed), plus لحم (meat, from m005 drumstick) and لسان (tongue, from m007 mouth) inferred from clip-art. Header word «ليمون» read by sight on m008.

items.detail: Dotted tracing grid (on m006/m013 teach card and on p002): rows of the dotted initial/isolated haa to trace, with grey directional arrows indicating right-to-left stroke direction; later columns add arrows, earlier ones are blank outline.

items.activity: writeLetter
items.detail: p002 top: 5 rows, instruction «صل غير النقاط واكتب حرف الهاء» — connect the dotted haa shapes then write the letter freehand on the baseline.

items.activity: fillBlank
items.detail: p002 middle: «ضع دائرة حول الحرف ( هـ / ـهـ / ـه )» — circle the correct form of haa (initial / medial / final) within the word bank below.

items.activity: connectWord
items.detail: p002 bottom word bank (two rows) of haa words for the circle/identify task: Row1 — مهدد، هاتف، سهم، هلال، وجه، فهد ; Row2 — هدية، نهر، هدى، هرة، كهف، مهر. Forms span initial (هاتف، هلال، هدية، هدى، هرة), medial (سهم، نهر، كهف، مهر، مهدد، فهد), and final (وجه).

items.activity: writeLetter
items.detail: p001: color-by-letter sorting sheet — instruction approx «لوّن حرف الهاء بالأحمر ...» (color the haa forms one color, distractor letters غ/ض/ظ other colors). Picture rows: phone (هاتف), cloud, frog (ضفدع), envelope (ظرف), boy's face — each tagged with a letter (هـ/ـهـ/ـه vs غ، ض، ظ) to identify and color.

items.activity: connectWord
items.detail: p003: «حوّط الصورة التي يحتوي اسمها على الحرف (هـ) ولوّنها» — circle the pictures whose name contains haa, then color. 3x3 grid: phone (هاتف), monkey, crescent moon (هلال), tiger/zebra, large ه letter, gift (هدية), arrow (سهم), pyramid (هرم), carrot (جزر).

perFile.notes: m007.wdp and m010.wdp are HD Photo (.wdp) format and could NOT be viewed/rendered — need separate conversion to confirm contents. No .wmf present. m006 and m013 are the same gift/هدية teach card (duplicate; m013 is a lower-res copy). Loose embedded clip-art (m001/m002/m015 phone, m003 frog, m004 envelope, m005 boy face, m008 monkey, m009 moon, m011 pyramid, m012 carrot, m014 hoopoe-bird) are vocabulary/coloring art for the p001-p003 picture exercises; several (frog, envelope, monkey, carrot) do NOT contain haa and serve as distractors or generic art. Header school name on p001-p003 read by sight as «مدرسة بن سعيد الابتدائية» (medium confidence). Word bank on p002 read by sight; «مهدد» and «مهر» are lower confidence (small print). p001 color-instruction distractor list (which color for which letter) is small print — medium confidence; distractor letters identified as غ/ض/ظ. Inferred picture-words (هرم pyramid, جزر carrot, هلال moon, سهم arrow) named by sight of the illustration.

perFile.instruction_en: Write/trace the letter haa.
perFile.notes: PDF counterpart of the haa packet. Treat as duplicate/alternate render of the haa letter packet; content should be reconciled with file 22 and confirmed by the mother.

perFile.instruction_en: Circle the picture that begins with the target letter; handwriting-first equivalent: write the initial letter for the picture.
perFile.notes: 28-page phonological-awareness set, one target letter per page. Paper task is picture discrimination; mapped to writeLetter from picture/sound. Picture words are not all labeled, so per-picture Arabic vocab is deferred to mother verification.
perFile.sourceFile: دائرة تميز صور الحروف- وعي صوتي.pdf

perFile.title: راء وعي صوتي
perFile.instruction_ar: لون الصورة التي تبدأ بالحرف ر
perFile.instruction_en: Color the picture that begins with the letter raa (ر)
perFile.notes: Phonological-awareness worksheet for letter ر. Content-bearing images: m004.jpg (instruction 'لون الصورة التي تبدأ بالحرف ر' with green ر letter and picture circles: sailboat قارب, man رجل, pillow مخدة, envelope رسالة, pomegranate رمان — child colors those starting with ر) and m001.jpg (composite of 4 labeled pictures: feather ريشة, man رجل, pomegranate رمان, plus one unlabeled curved shape top-right). Word labels in m001 read by sight; m004 picture words (قارب/مخدة/رسالة) inferred from the pictures since they are unlabeled circles. رسالة (envelope) and رمان and رجل are the correct ر-initial answers. m002.jpg is an unrelated English ABC sheet (M/N/O: octopus, newspaper, mouse, oval, needle, mailbox), watermarked superanimy.net — decorative/clip-art filler. m003.jpeg (rat), m005.jpeg (boy face), m006.gif (pomegranate outline), m007.gif (leg) are unlabeled coloring clip-art with no Arabic text. No .wdp/.wmf files present.

perFile.instruction_en: Connect the letter to the matching picture; handwriting-first equivalent: write the initial letter cued by one picture.
perFile.notes: Letter-picture matching worksheet. Good source for exercise family and picture cue usage; do not treat unlabeled image nouns as signed-off vocab until mother confirms.

perFile.instruction_en: Advanced grade-one workbook: write, complete, connect, and sentence-building style tasks.
perFile.notes: Large 158-page workbook. It was rendered locally, but this pass records it at inventory level only; detailed per-page Arabic transcription remains low-confidence/needs mother review because of volume and image-only pages.
perFile.sourceFile: كراس عمل للصف الأول (1) مرحلة متقدمة.pdf

perFile.title: كراس وعي صوتي
perFile.instruction_ar: انطق اسم الرسمة ثم اختر الصوت الأول واكتبه داخل المربع
perFile.instruction_en: Say the name of the picture, then choose the first sound and write it inside the box.
sentences: الجمل حيوان يعيش في الصحراء، وهو قادر على أن يتحمل الجوع والعطش.
الكلب حيوان نافع للإنسان، يساعده في الحراسة والصيد.
الضفادع تبيض ولا تلد، صوت الضفدع يسمى نقيقا.
النحلة تحب الزهرة، تأخذ منها الرحيق لتخرج لنا العسل.
perFile.notes: First-grade phonological-awareness workbook (كراسة تدريبات لغة عربية للصف الأول). Repetitive core layout pp.2-11: picture + 3 candidate letters + a box; child says the word, picks the first or last sound, writes it in the box. p2-6 = first sound (الصوت الأول); p8-11 = last sound (الصوت الأخير). pp.7,12 = same task but no candidate letters, just write the sound. pp.13-14 = identify long-vowel (mad: ا/و/ي) pictures. pp.15-16 = syllable/rhyme awareness (المقطع الساكن, الإيقاع اللفظي/سجع). pp.17-20 = write the missing syllable (اكتب المقطع المناسب) into partial words. p21 = place shadda over the correct letter. pp.22-23 = order scrambled syllables to form a correct word (رتب المقاطع). p24 = match number to picture (professions). pp.25-26 = complete/write sentence for the picture (verbs of رَاشِد). pp.27-28 = read passage and answer the question (camel/dog/frog/bee). p29 = crossword-style write-the-picture-name grid. pp.30-31 = first/last sound circles then write the word. Vocab and items captured by sight from clear pictures; less-certain final-sound words (pp.9-11, where the picture was small) flagged as lower confidence. No Arabic invented beyond what is legible; ambiguous picture names omitted rather than guessed.

perFile.instruction_en: Alphabet letter workbook: teach/trace/write one letter per page.
perFile.notes: 29-page alphabet workbook, likely cover plus one page per letter. Use as all-letter formation/tracing source; individual page vocab should be confirmed from images before promoting to signed content.

perFile.instruction_en: Stage 1 letter workbook: teach/trace/write one letter per page.
perFile.notes: 29-page stage-one letter workbook. Similar to file 29; all content signedOff:false pending mother review.

perFile.instruction_en: Circle the first letter you hear; handwriting-first equivalent: write the first letter heard.
perFile.notes: 12 image cards for phonological awareness. Paper action is circling; app mapping is writeLetter from sound. Specific audio/word prompts need mother confirmation.
perFile.sourceFile: لعبة وعي صوتي - ضع دائرة حول الحرف الاول الذي تسمعه.docx

perFile.instruction_en: Color the pictures that start with qaaf; handwriting-first equivalent: write ? for qaaf-starting pictures.
perFile.notes: Qaaf-focused phonological awareness/coloring worksheet. Picture nouns are image-only and should be verified before becoming vocab.
perFile.sourceFile: لون الصور التي تبدأ بحرف القاف.docx

perFile.instruction_en: Color the picture matching the letter; handwriting-first equivalent: write the letter cued by the image.
perFile.notes: 26-page letter-picture recognition/coloring set. Some letters may be missing or combined because page count is 26; flagged for mother review.

perFile.instruction_en: Color/discover letters in order; handwriting-first equivalent: trace/write the discovered target letters.
perFile.notes: Color-and-discover enrichment. Paper coloring maps to relaxed trace/write tasks per taxonomy; not a primary vocab source.
perFile.sourceFile: لون واكتشف- تلوين جميع الحروف بالترتيب لنحصل على صور.pdf

perFile.instruction_en: Letter maze; handwriting-first equivalent is relaxed tracing through/over the target letter path.
perFile.notes: 34-page maze/enrichment set. Map to no-fail relaxed traceLetter/letterMaze behavior; no vocab/sentences transcribed.

perFile.title: مفرد مثنى جمع
perFile.instruction_ar: حوّل الكلمة من المفرد إلى المثنى إلى الجمع
perFile.instruction_en: Transform the word from singular to dual to plural
perFile.notes: Worksheet has no explicit printed instruction line; instruction_ar is an inferred gloss of the singular→dual→plural exercise (flag for owner's mother to confirm wording). Structure: pages 1-5 are picture cards showing one/two/many of each object (no text); page 6 = singular word bank (مفرد), page 7 = dual word bank (مثنى), page 8 = plural word bank (جمع). Page 8 (plural) image is low-resolution; plural forms transcribed by matching column position across pages and by standard MSA plurals — verify especially: قَنانِيُّ (qanini), بَطاريق, مَقالِم, مَساطِر. The columns read right-to-left in 4 columns (8+8+8+3 = 27 triples). صوص (chick) / صيصان is colloquial; mother may prefer كَتْكوت/كَتاكيت. Vowel diacritics on duals/plurals partly inferred where the small image was unclear.

perFile.instruction_en: All-about-the-letter packet: identify, trace/write, and collect examples for each letter.
perFile.notes: 30-page all-about-letter packet. Useful source for per-letter presentation sections and possible vocab, but this pass does not promote unlabeled image nouns to signed vocab.

perFile.instruction_en: Curriculum/program overview workbook.
perFile.notes: 15-page curriculum overview/workbook. Treated as scope/context source rather than a direct exercise bank; needs mother review for any pedagogical sequencing decisions.

perFile.title: ن
perFile.instruction_en: Name (field at top of worksheet). Trace the letter noon following the dotted strokes and arrows.

items.activity: stroke-order demo: isolated noon (bowl + dot above) with directional arrows
traceLetter: top row, six dotted isolated noon bowls to trace, arrows guiding stroke direction
traceLetter: bottom row, six dotted initial/medial noon forms on the baseline to trace
teachCard: keyword picture (bee) illustrating noon
teachCard: keyword picture (palm tree) illustrating noon

perFile.notes: Primary worksheet is m002.jpeg (learnwithanas.blogspot.com). m003.wdp is an unviewable Windows Media Photo file - flagged, not read. m001.gif (cherries), m006.jpg (fire, labeled 'Fire' in English) appear to be unrelated/extra clip art not tied to the noon keyword. m004.png shows the word نَخْلة (palm tree) with palm illustration; m002 shows نَحْلة (bee) with bee illustration - both are valid noon keywords from different source pages bundled in this docx. Forms shown: isolated ن and initial/medial نـ. No full sentences present.

perFile.title: وعي صوتي حرف ال ج
perFile.instruction_en: Phonological awareness: the letter Jeem (ج). Identify pictures whose names contain the /j/ sound.
perFile.notes: Images are coloring-page clip-art line drawings; NO Arabic word labels are printed on any picture, so all Arabic words are inferred from what the picture depicts (not read), hence low/medium confidence. No sound-position markers shown in any image; 'initial' assigned by best-guess of the standard word. Image inventory: m001.jpg = English alphabet page (M/N/O letters with octopus/mouse/newspaper/mailbox/etc.) — decorative/irrelevant clip-art, NOT a ج item, excluded. m002 = chain (جنزير). m003 = giraffe — Arabic زرافة has NO ج (it has ز); likely NOT a ج item or a mis-embedded image — FLAGGED. m004 = rat/rodent (جرذ). m005 = circus elephant — Arabic فيل has NO ج; likely NOT a ج item — FLAGGED. m006 = mountain (جبل). m007 = carrot (جزر). m008 = camel (جمل). No .wdp/.wmf files present in folder. Overall: Arabic text extraction was garbled and no labels visible, so word assignments are inferred from pictures only.

perFile.title: وعي صوتي سجع
perFile.instruction_ar: مدوا خط بين الصورتين التي على نفس الوزن
perFile.instruction_en: Draw a line between the two pictures that have the same rhyme/weight (sound ending).

items.instruction_ar: السجع — مدوا خط بين الصورتين التي على نفس الوزن
items.note: Arabic words inferred from pictures, not printed; line-matching by ending sound. كلب/قلب and علم/قلم rhyme strongly; نار/فأر plausible; طائرة/سيارة a remaining pair.

items.instruction_ar: السجع — مدوا خط بين الصورتين التي على نفس الوزن
items.note: Inferred picture words. خروف/صوف and جمل/جبل rhyme; جوز/لوز plausible (final sound). Left 'بنت' (girl face) pairing to فأس (pickaxe) is unclear — low confidence on that pair.

items.instruction_ar: ضع إشارة ✓ اذا تشابهت الصور بالسجع ( على نفس الوزن )
items.note: Checkbox task: mark ✓ if the two pictures rhyme. قلم/علم rhyme (check); جبل/جمل rhyme (check). Others (فستان/غابة, بطة/شجرة, قطة/قمر) do not clearly rhyme. Picture words inferred.

items.instruction_ar: مدوا خط بين الصورتين التي على نفس الوزن
items.note: Picture identifications uncertain; intended rhyme pairings not confidently readable from images. Left col bottom (tangled threads) and several right-col items ambiguous. Low confidence on this page's pairings.

items.instruction_ar: لائم بين الصورتين اللتان لهما نفس الإيقاع أو الوزن :
items.note: Match pictures with same rhythm/weight. بيت/زيت rhyme clearly. Other intended pairs (جرس, سيارة, كوب vs حصان, طائرة, فستان) inferred; جرس/حصان and كوب could pair variously — uncertain.

items.instruction_ar: مد خطا بين صورتين لهما نفس القافية
items.note: Match two pictures with same rhyme (قافية). Picture identifications and intended pairings not confidently readable; right column has an arrow graphic suggesting a worked example. Low confidence on specific pairs.

perFile.notes: Phonological-awareness rhyme (سجع) worksheet, first grade, by د. ناديا أبو طعمة. All printed INSTRUCTIONS are clearly legible and given in real Arabic by sight. The exercises are picture-matching (draw a line / put a check between pictures that rhyme on the same ending/weight); NO Arabic words are printed beside the pictures — every word in leftColumn/rightColumn/rhymeGroups is INFERRED from the drawn picture and is a best-guess identification, not transcribed text. Confidence high on instructions, lower on inferred picture words and intended pairings (esp. p005 and p007). worksheetType set to 'writeWord' as the closest fit (word-level rhyme task), though the child draws lines/checks rather than writing. signedOff:false.

perFile.title: وعي صوتي لجميع الحروف
perFile.instruction_ar: اكتب الحرف الذي تبدأ به كل صورة
perFile.instruction_en: Write the letter that each picture begins with (phonological awareness: identify the target sound in each pictured word).

items.note: pomegranate; alef as internal long vowel
pen pictured; no baa — likely a distractor/non-example
gift pictured; no baa — likely distractor
lightbulb pictured; haa not khaa — distractor
washing machine pictured; no daal — distractor
car pictured; no emphatic taa — distractor
blank paper pictured; no ayn — distractor
blank paper pictured; no laam — distractor

perFile.notes: Phonological-awareness flashcard set: one card per Arabic letter (28 letters across pages 1-7; page 8 is an answer/label key strip). Each card shows the target letter plus 3 pictures; the child writes/identifies the target letter and decides which pictured word(s) contain its sound. Cards mix correct examples with distractors, so many items carry position 'none' (target sound absent). Word identities were cross-checked against the labeled Arabic key strip on p008 by sight. Some letters (ض, ظ, ك, ل etc.) had a blank picture cell in the card grid, so fewer than 3 picture-words appear for those. Vocabulary read by sight; common kid nouns. Several distractor mappings are inferred (which 3 pictures sit on which card) and could vary; the correct-example word per letter is high confidence, the distractor assignments are medium confidence. p008 dense answer strip Arabic labels partially small — used for confirmation, not all individually legible.
